question: [File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/service/TokenAirdropService.java -> Scope: Critical: Unintended permanent freezing of funds'] [Function: getAirdrops(TokenAirdropRequest)] Can an unprivileged external user craft a TokenAirdropRequest with a non-existent accountId such that EntityServiceImpl.lookup() throws EntityNotFoundException, permanently preventing the receiver from ever discovering their PENDING airdrop and causing the airdropped funds to remain frozen in PENDING state indefinitely?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/service/TokenAirdropService.java -> Scope: Critical: Unintended permanent freezing of funds'] [Function: getAirdrops(TokenAirdropRequest)] Can an unprivileged external user supply an EntityIdEvmAddressParameter that resolves to a different account than the true receiver, causing the receiver to receive an empty result set from getAirdrops() and never claim their PENDING airdrop, effectively freezing the airdropped tokens permanently?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/service/TokenAirdropService.java -> Scope: Critical: Unintended permanent freezing of funds'] [Function: getAirdrops(TokenAirdropRequest)] Can an unprivileged external user submit a request using EntityIdAliasParameter with a crafted alias that maps to a different EntityId than the intended receiver, causing getAirdrops() to return airdrops belonging to another account and leaving the real receiver's PENDING airdrops undiscoverable and permanently frozen?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/service/TokenAirdropService.java -> Scope: Critical: Unintended permanent freezing of funds'] [Function: getAirdrops(TokenAirdropRequest)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that TokenAirdropRepositoryCustomImpl.findAll() hardcodes STATE = AirdropState.PENDING to cause a scenario where a legitimate airdrop whose state was never transitioned from PENDING (due to a failed upsert) is permanently visible as PENDING but uncollectable, freezing the funds?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/service/TokenAirdropService.java -> Scope: Critical: Unintended permanent freezing of funds'] [Function: getAirdrops(TokenAirdropRequest)] Can an unprivileged external user manipulate the Bound parameters (entityIds, tokenIds, serialNumbers) in a TokenAirdropRequest such that getBounds() returns a bound list that excludes specific PENDING airdrops from the SQL query results, causing those airdrops to be invisible to the receiver and the funds to remain frozen?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/service/TokenAirdropService.java -> Scope: Critical: Unintended permanent freezing of funds'] [Function: getAirdrops(TokenAirdropRequest)] Can an unprivileged external user send a request with a limit of 1 and craft pagination cursor parameters (receiverId, senderId, tokenId, serialNumber) such that the generated next-page link in linkFactory.create() skips over specific PENDING airdrops, causing those airdrops to never appear in any page and the funds to remain permanently frozen?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/service/TokenAirdropService.java -> Scope: Critical: Unintended permanent freezing of funds'] [Function: getAirdrops(TokenAirdropRequest)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the DEFAULT_SERIAL_VALUE = 0L check in TokenAirdropsController.getSort() so that when the last airdrop in a page has serialNumber == 0 (fungible token), the sort omits SERIAL_NUMBER, causing the next-page link to skip NFT airdrops with the same tokenId but non-zero serial numbers, permanently hiding those PENDING NFT airdrops?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/service/TokenAirdropService.java -> Scope: Critical: Unintended permanent freezing of funds'] [Function: getAirdrops(TokenAirdropRequest)] Can an unprivileged external user craft a Bound with a GT lower bound equal to Long.MAX_VALUE - 1 such that getAdjustedLowerRangeValue() overflows to Long.MIN_VALUE, producing an invalid SQL condition that either returns no results or crashes the query, causing all PENDING airdrops for the account to be inaccessible and funds frozen?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/service/TokenAirdropService.java -> Scope: Critical: Unintended permanent freezing of funds'] [Function: getAirdrops(TokenAirdropRequest)] Can an unprivileged external user supply a LT upper bound of 1 to a Bound such that adjustUpperBound() returns 0 (1 - 1), and when combined with a lower bound of 0, the condition excludes all valid token IDs or serial numbers, causing the query to return no PENDING airdrops and leaving funds permanently frozen?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/service/TokenAirdropService.java -> Scope: Critical: Unintended permanent freezing of funds'] [Function: getAirdrops(TokenAirdropRequest)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the Bound constructor's primarySortField check (adjustedLower > adjustedUpper throws IllegalArgumentException) by crafting a range where the lower bound is just above the upper bound, causing the service to throw an exception and preventing the receiver from ever querying their PENDING airdrops, effectively freezing the funds?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/service/TokenAirdropService.java -> Scope: Critical: Unintended permanent freezing of funds'] [Function: getAirdrops(TokenAirdropRequest)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the role reversal in AirdropRequestType (OUTSTANDING uses SENDER_ACCOUNT_ID as base, PENDING uses RECEIVER_ACCOUNT_ID as base) to query the wrong endpoint, receiving empty results and never discovering their PENDING airdrops, causing funds to remain frozen?
